mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects expanding household income and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns following payroll expansions is less than 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the Rank IC is not negative over a 2-year window within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) and decrease in industrial production (INDPRO) signals demand-driven margin compression, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases month-over-month while INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform the linear combination of individual RSAFS and INDPRO signals (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window, or the negative return hit rate is below 55% when the divergence occurs.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds 6% and rises further, labor market deterioration triggers recessionary equity pricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 6% and increasing over prior month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6% and is rising, or the Rank IC is not negative and significant at the 10% level in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in housing starts (HOUST) anticipate forward construction spending and durable goods demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the frequency of positive 60-day forward returns following HOUST increases is less than 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month industrial production growth exceeding 0.5% indicates accelerating capacity utilization and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) following months when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5%, or the positive return hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% combined with rising unemployment signals a policy-induced slowdown, jointly forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond either effect alone.
regime_hint: restrictive policy with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction term does not produce more negative returns than the best univariate signal (Diebold-Mariano test p > 0.05) over a 3-year window, or the negative return hit rate falls below 55% when the joint condition is active.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals aggregate output expansion that supports corporate revenue growth, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the proportion of positive 60-day forward returns following GDP growth is less than 55 percent.
